title: GGUF Q6_K superblock dequant is now a Form recipe — four-way proven, bit-exact vs gguf on real llama weights
advances: form-native-models.form rung (1) toward full real-model inference, completing the quant-type coverage: llama3.2:3b uses exactly three tensor types — F32 (no dequant), Q4_K (q4k-dequant.fk, PR #3226, 168 tensors), and Q6_K (29 tensors, including token_embd.weight and output.weight). Q6_K was the last quant type whose unpack was carried by python's gguf.quants.dequantize. With this recipe every weight in the real model now loads to f32 through a Form recipe, not a borrowed library.
device: Apple M4 Max (40-core GPU), arm64 Darwin 26.3.1 — proof is host-independent (pure arithmetic band)
fixture: a REAL llama3.2:3b Q6_K superblock — token_embd.weight (the model's actual embedding table), first 210-byte block: f16->f32 d=0.000011682510375976562, ql[128], qh[64], scales[16] (signed int8)
recipe: form/form-stdlib/q6k-dequant.fk — the per-half walk w = d * scale * ((ql_nibble | ((qh>>2g)&3)<<4) - 32), every bit-op expressed as an integer-arithmetic identity (& = mod, >> = div, << = mul, | of disjoint fields = add, qh-shift = div by 4^g), so the band proves on the four-way arithmetic floor with NO bitwise primitive. The one new wrinkle vs Q4_K's unsigned 6-bit scales: Q6_K's scales are SIGNED int8 — q6k-s8 carries the sign (byte >= 128 -> byte - 256).
band: form/form-stdlib/tests/q6k-dequant-band.fk — 12 sample indices spanning both halves (h=0/1), all four sub-groups (g=0..3 -> qh shifts 0/2/4/6, scale offsets +0/+2/+4/+6), both nibble halves (low ql&0xF for g<2, high ql>>4 for g>=2), both scale-halves (is = l/16), and signed scales; expected values are round(w*1e6) from gguf.quants.dequantize, the last via the full 256-list walk
reference: transcription of llama.cpp dequantize_row_q6_K, cross-checked against gguf.quants.dequantize on the same block bytes: max_abs_diff = 0.0 (bit-exact). The kernel's f64 arithmetic rounds identically to gguf's f32 on every sampled index. The band's expected ints are this authoritative reference.
proof: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/q6k-dequant.fk form-stdlib/tests/q6k-dequant-band.fk -> 4095, fourth arm: 1 band four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables), 1 ok, 0 divergent
verdict: FOUR-WAY 4095, 0 divergent — Go, Rust, TypeScript, and the emitted fkwu walker all reproduce the gguf reference dequant of a real Q6_K superblock, bit-for-bit
reading: Together with q4k-dequant.fk and f16-decode.fk, the dequant MATH for every quant type the real llama3.2:3b uses is now Form-native and four-way verified against the canonical gguf reference. d enters as f32 (decoded by the proven f16-decode.fk); the byte-reading carrier (read_file_bytes) remains host I/O. A complete real-model weight load is now a Form recipe end to end. Remaining toward full real-model inference: the full transformer block (attention/softmax/SwiGLU) on the GPU — already training there per metal_attn_audit.sh / metal_ffn_audit.sh — then the generation loop (tokenizer + KV cache + greedy decode).
reproduce: venv with gguf+numpy; read token_embd.weight first 210 bytes from ~/.ollama/models/blobs (llama3.2:3b); the band fixture is those bytes; validate.sh four-way == 4095
note: The fourth-arm flattener requires plain-decimal float literals, not scientific notation — an e-notation d (1.16e-05) computed -5 on fkwu while the three walkers gave 11215; the same value as a plain decimal (0.000011682...) crosses four-way. Worth lifting into the AUTHORING guide if it recurs.
